title: Zhengzhou University March 2026 Admissions: Scholarships & MBBS
content:
Admissions Open — March 2026 Priority Deadline: 15 Feb 2026 ⏳ calculating…

Zhengzhou University (ZZU) March 2026 Intake

CWUR Rank 259 · Zhengzhou, Henan (China). Offering scholarships up to 100% tuition-free for select majors and a renowned BMDC-listed MBBS program.

Program Snapshot & Scholarships 🎓

Foundation Program (Mar–Jul 2026)

Tuition: 5,000 CNY
Hostel: 2,500 CNY (Off-campus options available)

Bachelor's Scholarship Categories

Category 1: 100% Free Tuition

  • International Economics & Trade
  • Tourism Management

Category 2: 5,000 CNY/Year

  • Civil Engineering
  • Pharmacy
  • Software Engineering

Category 3: MBBS 🩺

Fee: 36,000 CNY/Year
(BMDC Listed)

Estimated Additional Fees 💰

ItemAmount (CNY)
Application800 (non-refundable)
Tuition (Standard)23,000–36,000 / year
Hostel5,000 / year
Insurance1,000 / year
Medical Check (1st yr)100–600
Residence Permit430 / year

Application Process

Counseling & Course Match: Review profile and shortlist programs.
Document Preparation: Prepare all required documents as per the ZZU checklist.
Offer & Visa: Await the university's decision and proceed with visa guidance.

Eligibility

  • HSC/A-Level or equivalent.
  • Valid passport and academic transcripts.
  • Financial proof & medical check.
  • Age Limit: 18-25 at enrollment.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Who qualifies for 100% tuition scholarships?
Students with strong academic profiles applying for International Economics & Trade or Tourism Management. The final decision rests with ZZU.
Is the MBBS program BMDC-listed?
Yes, the MBBS program is BMDC-listed, with an annual fee of 36,000 CNY under the scholarship scheme.
What is the application timeline?
The Foundation program runs from March to July 2026. The priority application deadline is 15 Feb 2026.

title: China K Visa for STEM: A Guide for Bangladeshi Graduates
content:
China's New K Visa: A Guide for STEM Graduates

Great news for aspiring tech professionals! China has launched the K Visa, a brand-new category aimed at attracting global talent in STEM. For recent graduates and researchers in Bangladesh, this opens an exciting pathway to one of the world's most innovative ecosystems.

What is the New K Visa? 🔬

The K Visa is designed to be more flexible and accommodating than previous options. It specifically targets the world's brightest minds to fuel China's rapid, innovation-driven growth. Key benefits include:

  • No Employer Sponsorship: You don't need a job offer to apply.
  • Greater Flexibility: The visa supports education, research, and even starting your own business.
  • Multiple Entries & Extended Stays: It offers options for multiple-entry and longer validity periods, providing more stability.

Are You Eligible? Checklist for Applicants ✅

  • Education: You need a Bachelor’s degree or higher in a STEM field from a recognized university.
  • Professional Status: You should be a recent graduate or a professional currently involved in STEM-related research or education.

High-Demand Target Fields

Artificial Intelligence (AI)
Data Science
Renewable Energy
Computer Science
Mechanical Engineering
Biotechnology

Your Document Toolkit: What You'll Need 📋

  • Valid Passport (with at least six months of validity and two blank pages).
  • Recent Passport Photo (48x33 mm with a white background).
  • COVA Certificate (printed from the online system).
  • Academic Proof (STEM degree certificates and an updated CV).
  • Proof of Innovation (patents, publications, or proof of relevant work).
  • Financial Records (6-month bank statements or employer letter).
  • Medical Reports (chest X-ray, health screening, blood tests).

Important Note

All documents in Bengali must be professionally translated into English.

How to Apply: A Step-by-Step Guide 🗺️

The application is a two-part process: an online submission followed by an in-person interview in Dhaka.

Part 1: Online Application (COVA)

Go to the COVA website: cova.mfa.gov.cn
Fill out the form, selecting "K Visa" as the category.
Upload digital copies of all your documents.
Pay the fees online and receive confirmation.

Part 2: Physical Submission (Dhaka)

After online approval, print your certificate and visit one of the locations below for your interview and fingerprinting.

  • Interview Hours: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM
  • Embassy: Plot 2 & 4, Embassy Road, Baridhara
  • Visa Centre: Praasad Trade Centre, 6 Kemal Ataturk Ave, Banani

Understanding the Costs 💰

Visa TypeFee (BDT)
Single-entry2,400
Double-entry3,600
Multiple-entry (6 mo)4,800
Multiple-entry (12 mo)7,200

Mandatory Service Fee: 4,520 BDT (incl. tax)
Example Total Cost (Single-entry): 2,400 + 4,520 = 6,920 BDT

Pro-Tips for Success 🧭

  • Prepare documents and translations early.
  • Double-check all information on the COVA form.
  • Arrive early for your interview appointment.
  • Keep digital and physical copies of everything.
  • Track your application status on the COVA portal.
metaDescription: China launches the new K Visa for STEM graduates and professionals. Learn about the eligibility, documents, and application process for Bangladeshis. No employer sponsorship required.
author: Admin

title: Study in South Korea 2025 - Complete Guide for Bangladeshi Students
content:
🇰🇷 South Korea KGSP Scholarship Available EAP/KAP Programs

Study in South Korea 2025 - Complete Guide for Bangladeshi Students

Discover world-class education in South Korea with cutting-edge technology programs, K-culture immersion, and generous scholarship opportunities. Get expert guidance for Korean Government Scholarship (KGSP) and university applications.

Why Choose South Korea for Higher Education? 🎓

  • World-Class Universities: SNU, KAIST, Yonsei rank among top global institutions
  • Technology Excellence: Leading in AI, robotics, and digital innovation
  • Generous Scholarships: KGSP covers tuition, living expenses, and airfare
  • Cultural Experience: Immerse in K-culture, K-pop, and Korean traditions
  • Career Opportunities: Work with Samsung, LG, Hyundai, and global tech companies
  • Safe Environment: One of the safest countries for international students

Top Universities in South Korea 🏛️

SKY Universities

  • Seoul National University (SNU)
  • Korea University
  • Yonsei University

Specialized Universities

  • KAIST (Science & Technology)
  • Hanyang University
  • Sungkyunkwan University

Korean Government Scholarship Program (KGSP) 💰

KGSP Benefits

  • Full Tuition Coverage: 100% tuition fee waiver
  • Monthly Stipend: 900,000 KRW (≈$700) for undergraduate, 1,000,000 KRW (≈$780) for graduate
  • Airfare: Round-trip economy class ticket
  • Korean Language Training: 1-year intensive Korean course
  • Medical Insurance: Comprehensive health coverage
  • Research Support: Additional funding for research activities

KGSP Application Timeline

February-March: Application period opens
April-May: Document review and interviews
June-July: Final selection results
September: Korean language training begins
March (Next Year): University enrollment

Popular Programs 📚

  • Computer Science & AI
  • Engineering (All fields)
  • Business Administration
  • Korean Language (EAP/KAP)
  • Medicine & Healthcare
  • Arts & Design
  • International Studies

Language Requirements 🌐

Korean Programs

  • TOPIK Level 3+ (minimum)
  • TOPIK Level 4+ (preferred)

English Programs

  • IELTS 6.0+ or TOEFL 80+
  • Some programs accept TOEFL 70+

Cost of Education 💵

ItemCost (USD)
Tuition (per year)$3,000 - $8,000
Living Expenses$4,000 - $7,000
Accommodation$2,000 - $4,000
Food & Transport$2,000 - $3,000

Application Process & Requirements 📋

Required Documents

  • Academic transcripts (HSC/A-Level)
  • Language proficiency certificate
  • Health certificate
  • Financial statement ($10,000+ proof)
  • Passport copy
  • Application form
  • Personal statement
  • Recommendation letters

Visa Requirements

  • Certificate of Admission (COA)
  • Financial documents
  • Passport copy
  • Visa application form
  • Health insurance
  • Passport photos

Student Life in South Korea 🎌

Cultural Experience

  • K-pop and K-drama culture
  • Traditional Korean festivals
  • Delicious Korean cuisine
  • Beautiful temples and palaces
  • Modern cities with ancient history

Career Opportunities

  • Internship opportunities at major companies
  • Part-time work during studies
  • Post-graduation work visa options
  • Global career prospects
  • Alumni network worldwide

Ready to Study in South Korea? 🇰🇷

Get expert guidance for KGSP scholarship applications and university admissions. Our team has helped 100+ Bangladeshi students secure scholarships in South Korea.
metaDescription: Complete guide to study in South Korea 2025. Get KGSP scholarship assistance, university applications, and expert guidance for Bangladeshi students. Free consultation available.

title: Study in Croatia 2025 - EU Education with Adriatic Coast Beauty
content:
🇭🇷 Croatia 🇪🇺 EU Member Affordable Education Adriatic Coast

Study in Croatia 2025 - EU Education with Adriatic Coast Beauty

Experience affordable EU education in Croatia with stunning Adriatic coastline, rich cultural heritage, and excellent scholarship opportunities. Get expert guidance for Croatian universities and EU study programs.

Why Choose Croatia for Higher Education? 🎓

  • EU Member Benefits: Study in EU with Schengen visa access
  • Affordable Education: Low tuition fees compared to other EU countries
  • Beautiful Location: Stunning Adriatic coastline and historic cities
  • English Programs: Many courses taught in English
  • Cultural Heritage: Rich history and UNESCO World Heritage sites
  • Safe Environment: One of the safest countries in Europe
  • Career Opportunities: Work rights in EU after graduation

Top Universities in Croatia 🏛️

Major Universities

  • University of Zagreb
  • University of Split
  • University of Rijeka
  • University of Dubrovnik

Specialized Institutions

  • Josip Juraj Strossmayer University
  • University of Zadar
  • University of Pula
  • Polytechnic of Zagreb

EU Education Benefits 🇪🇺

Why Study in EU?

  • Schengen Visa Access: Travel freely across 26 European countries
  • EU Work Rights: Work anywhere in EU after graduation
  • Quality Education: EU-standard education system
  • Recognition: Degrees recognized worldwide
  • Research Opportunities: Access to EU research programs
  • Cultural Exchange: Diverse international student community

Scholarship Opportunities

Croatian Government Scholarship: Full or partial tuition coverage
Erasmus+ Program: EU exchange and mobility programs
University Scholarships: Merit-based financial aid
Bilateral Agreements: Country-specific scholarship programs
Research Grants: Funding for research projects

Popular Programs 📚

  • Engineering & Technology
  • Medicine & Healthcare
  • Business & Economics
  • Arts & Humanities
  • Tourism & Hospitality
  • Marine Sciences
  • Computer Science
  • Environmental Studies

Language Requirements 🌐

English Programs

  • IELTS 6.0+ or TOEFL 80+
  • Some programs accept TOEFL 70+

Croatian Programs

  • Croatian language certificate
  • Language preparation courses available

Cost of Education 💵

ItemCost (EUR)
Tuition (per year)€2,000 - €6,000
Living Expenses€3,000 - €5,000
Accommodation€1,500 - €3,000
Food & Transport€1,500 - €2,000

Application Process & Requirements 📋

Required Documents

  • Academic transcripts (HSC/A-Level)
  • Language proficiency certificate
  • Health certificate
  • Financial statement (€5,000+ proof)
  • Passport copy
  • Motivation letter
  • Recommendation letters
  • Application form

Visa Requirements

  • Admission letter from university
  • Financial documents
  • Passport copy
  • Health insurance
  • Accommodation proof
  • Visa application form
  • Passport photos

Student Life in Croatia 🏖️

Cultural Experience

  • Beautiful Adriatic coastline
  • Historic cities like Dubrovnik and Split
  • UNESCO World Heritage sites
  • Rich Croatian cuisine
  • Traditional festivals and events
  • Mediterranean climate

Career Opportunities

  • EU work rights after graduation
  • Tourism and hospitality industry
  • Technology and innovation sectors
  • Marine and environmental sciences
  • International business opportunities
  • Research and academic positions

Ready to Study in Croatia? 🇭🇷

Get expert guidance for Croatian university applications and scholarship opportunities. Our team has helped 50+ Bangladeshi students secure admissions in EU universities.

author: EduExpress International

title: 2026 CSCA Complete Guide: Your Path to Study in China
content:
2026 CSCA Complete Guide: Your Path to Study in China

Your essential guide to the China Scholastic Competency Assessment (CSCA) – China's new national academic test for international undergraduate admissions, starting in 2026. This is your first step to Study in China.

🌍 What is CSCA?

The China Scholastic Competency Assessment (CSCA) is China’s newly established national academic test for international undergraduate admissions to Study in China.

Starting from 2026, all applicants to Chinese Government Scholarship universities will need to take the CSCA before submitting their applications to Study in China.

📈 By 2028, CSCA is expected to become a mandatory admission requirement for all undergraduate programs in China for those who wish to Study in China.

Official Statement

China officially launches the "China Scholastic Competency Assessment (CSCA)" from 2026, establishing a unified and authoritative undergraduate admission standard to evaluate international students' academic level and learning potential.

🧭 I. Test System Overview

📘 Test Structure for Study in China

The CSCA covers four main subjects, designed to assess core academic skills for students who wish to Study in China:

  • Specialized Chinese (for Chinese-taught programs)
  • Mathematics (compulsory) - *Includes Algebra, Geometry, and introductory Calculus concepts aligning with Chinese high school (Grade 12) curriculum.*
  • Physics (optional)
  • Chemistry (optional)

Specialized Chinese Test Breakdown:

  • Character recognition
  • Cloze tests
  • Word distinction
  • Paragraph construction
  • Sentence completion
  • Reading comprehension

The Specialized Chinese exam focuses on comprehensive language ability, including question types such as character recognition, word distinction, sentence completion, and reading comprehension. For English-taught programs, this section is not required.

Exemption Rules for Study in China

  • Applicants for English-taught programs are exempt from the Specialized Chinese test.
  • Those majoring in Chinese Language may be exempt if holding a valid HSK Level 4 certificate. An HSK Level 4 demonstrates a good command of basic Chinese, capable of discussing a wide range of topics.

This structure ensures that the test is relevant to the student's chosen program, while maintaining a core academic assessment for those aspiring to Study in China.

🕐 II. Test Schedule & Global Coverage

From 2026 onward, CSCA exams will be conducted five times per year for those who want to Study in China:

  • January
  • March
  • April
  • June
  • December

Regional Focus for Study in China Applicants

  • Asia-Pacific: April, June, December
  • Europe & Americas: January, March

To make the test more accessible, China will build offline centers in major source countries such as India, South Korea, Japan, Malaysia, and Kazakhstan, aiming for full coverage in the top 20 countries within 2–3 years for future students eager to Study in China.

Starting from 2026, CSCA will be held regularly 5 times a year, covering major regions globally by time zone, and establishing offline test centers in key source countries to achieve internationalization and standardization of the exam.

🏫 III. Admission Policy & Score Use for Study in China

All Chinese Government Scholarship universities must clearly state in their 2026/2027 admission guides that the CSCA Score Report is a required document for undergraduate applications to Study in China.

How Universities Will Use CSCA Scores for Study in China:

  • Receive applicants’ official scores directly from the CSCA system.
  • Use results as a quantitative measure of academic readiness.
  • Set its own admission cut-off lines based on internal standards and applicant pool quality for students hoping to Study in China.

Each university can set its own admission score lines based on its professional requirements, and understand the applicant's relative position globally through score rankings.

💻 IV. Registration & Test Fees to Study in China

🗓️ Registration Time

  • Expected registration period: November 2025
  • Conducted primarily through the official website: www.csca.cn
  • Available in Chinese and English.
  • Offline registration assistance will be available at authorized overseas centers in regions with limited internet access for those aspiring to Study in China.

💰 Test Fees for Study in China

Item Amount (RMB)
Single subject 450
Two or more subjects 700

Payment supports: Alipay, WeChat Pay, UnionPay, VISA, and MasterCard.

Registration is expected to open in November 2025, primarily online, and various global payment methods are supported.

📊 V. Scoring & Results for Study in China

  • All subjects are scored on a 100-point scale.
  • Results serve as reference data for universities’ admission decisions for those who want to Study in China.
  • Scores from online and computer-based tests are released within 7 working days.
  • Paper-based test results are available within 14 working days.
  • Universities can also view global percentile rankings to evaluate candidates’ international academic standing when they apply to Study in China.

CSCA results will be announced within one to two weeks after the exam, and a global percentile ranking will be provided to help universities accurately select talent.

📚 VI. Preparation Tips to Study in China

  • Strengthen your basics: Study subjects at Chinese high school (Grade 12) level to match academic standards required to Study in China.
  • Take mock exams: Use the “Study in China” Entrance Exam Mock Test to experience typical Chinese test styles and question formats.
  • Use study materials: Purchase reference books related to mathematics, physics, chemistry, and academic Chinese for your journey to Study in China.
  • Join foundation programs: Enroll in preparatory or pre-university courses at Chinese universities to strengthen your readiness to Study in China.

It is recommended to systematically study and practice with mock tests in advance, familiarize yourself with the question types and exam interface, and boost your confidence for the exam.

🌟 Conclusion: Your Opportunity to Study in China

The CSCA marks a significant milestone in the modernization and standardization of China’s international admissions system for those who want to Study in China.

For students aiming to pursue a Bachelor’s degree in China, CSCA is not just an exam — it’s your gateway to scholarship opportunities and top-tier universities.

🚀 Start preparing now for the 2026 CSCA — your first step toward Study in China!

📌 Official Website: www.csca.cn 📅 First Global Test: December 21, 2025 📘 Registration Opens: November 2025

For personalized guidance on the CSCA and studying in China, contact EduExpress International today.
metaDescription: Planning to Study in China? Learn everything about the new China Scholastic Competency Assessment (CSCA) starting 2026. This guide covers test structure, schedule, fees, scoring, and preparation tips for international students aiming to Study in China.
author: Admin

  303. National Police University of China中国刑事警察学院
  304. China Medical University中国医科大学
  305. China Conservatory of Music中国音乐学院
  306. Minzu University of China中央民族大学
Contact Our Consultants

title: Zhejiang University of Technology (ZJUT) March 2026 Chinese Language Program
content:
Admissions Open — March 2026 Deadline: 30 Dec 2025 ⏳ calculating…

Zhejiang University of Technology (ZJUT) March 2026 Intake

🏫 University: Zhejiang University of Technology (ZJUT)
📍 Location: Hangzhou City, Zhejiang Province
🌍 World Ranking: 301–400 | 🇨🇳 National Ranking: 62
👥 Age Range: 16–55

Program Options: Chinese Language 📚

Begin your Chinese learning journey at ZJUT! Choose your perfect study duration from the options below. The March 2026 intake starts in early March.

Option 1: One Semester Program

An intensive, 5-month program perfect for beginners or those looking for a short-term immersion experience.

  • Duration: Mar 2026 – Jul 2026
  • Tuition: 9,000 CNY
  • Total Estimated Cost: ~13,700 CNY

Option 2: One Year Program

A comprehensive two-semester program designed to significantly advance your Chinese language proficiency.

  • Duration: Two Semesters
  • Tuition: 18,000 CNY
  • Total Estimated Cost: ~26,500 CNY

Estimated Fees Structure 💰

One Semester Program

ItemAmount (CNY)
Tuition9,000
Accommodation (5 months)3,000
Application Fee400
Health Check-up~500
Visa Extension400
Insurance400
Total Est. Cost~13,700

One Year Program

ItemAmount (CNY)
Tuition (2 semesters)18,000
Accommodation (10 months)6,000
Application Fee400
Health Check-up~500
Visa Extension (2 times)800
Insurance (2 semesters)800
Total Est. Cost~26,500

Required Documents 📄

Passport
Photo
High School Certificate & Transcript
Health Check-up Report
English Proficiency Certificate
Non-Criminal Record / Police Clearance
Bank Statement (min. $5,000 USD)

Important Notes & Eligibility 📌

  • Deposit: A 1,400 CNY (non-refundable) deposit is due after you receive the Pre-admission Letter. This includes the 400 CNY application fee and a 1,000 CNY tuition deposit.
  • Accommodation: On-campus rooms are limited and allocated on a first-come, first-served basis.
  • Age Limit: Applicants must be between 16 and 55 years old.
  • Deadlines:
    • March 2026 Intake: 30 December 2025
    • September Intake: 30th July
  • Disclaimer: All fees are subject to slight adjustment by the university.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

What program options are available?
ZJUT offers two main options for the Chinese language program: a one-semester (5-month) program and a one-year (two-semester) program.
What is the application deadline?
The deadline for the March 2026 intake is 30 December 2025. There is also a 30th July deadline for the September intake.
What is the deposit requirement?
After receiving a pre-admission letter, a non-refundable deposit of 1,400 CNY is required. This covers the 400 CNY application fee and a 1,000 CNY tuition deposit to secure your place.
metaDescription: Apply for the March 2026 Chinese Language Program at Zhejiang University of Technology (ZJUT). One-semester and one-year options available. Deadline: 30 Dec 2025.

title: Civil Aviation University of China (CAUC) March 2026 Foundation Program & Scholarship
content:
Admissions Open — March 2026 Deadline: Jan 20, 2026 ⏳ calculating…

Civil Aviation University of China (CAUC) March 2026 Intake

🏫 University: Civil Aviation University of China (CAUC)
📍 Location: Tianjin, China
📚 Program: Half-Year Foundation Program (Starts March 2026)
🎓 Benefit: Pathway to Full First-Class Scholarships for Bachelor's degrees.

Program Snapshot: Foundation & Scholarship Pathway 🎓

Foundation Program (March 2026)

This half-year program prepares students for bachelor's level study. Age limit: 18-30.

Core Courses:

  • HSK Courses & Chinese Culture
  • English Courses
  • Math & Physics
  • General Education of International Civil Aviation
  • Entry-level practice for Civil Aviation Industry

Pathway to Bachelor's Degree

After passing the foundation exams, students can be admitted to English-taught bachelor's programs with scholarships:

  • Communication Engineering
  • Bachelor in Business Administration (BBA)

Note: Passing with HSK-3/HSK-5 grants eligibility for First-Class scholarships in English or Chinese-taught programs.

1st Year Bachelor's Scholarship Details 🏆

First Class Scholarship

  • Full Tuition fees free
  • Full Accommodation fees free
  • Monthly Stipend 1400 CNY

Second Class Scholarship

  • Full Tuition fees free
  • Full Accommodation fees free

Fees during Bachelor Degree (with scholarship): Scholarship recipients are still responsible for Insurance (800 CNY/Year), Medical Checkup (400 CNY/1st Year), and Visa Fees (400 CNY/Year).

Foundation Program Fees 💰

ItemAmount (CNY)Notes
Application Fees400Pay before application (non-refundable)
Tuition Fees7,500Pay after receiving admission letter
Accommodation Fees3,000Per semester (Double Bed), pay upon arrival
Insurance Fees800Per year
Medical Checkup400–600One-time fee

Application Process & Documents 📄

Required Documents

Passport
Photo
Highest Educational Transcript & Certificate
Medical Check up
English Proficiency (IELTS 5.5+, TOEFL 70+, or Duolingo 85+)
Non-Criminal Record
Resume (CV)
3-minute self-introduction video (in English)

Important Notes & Eligibility

  • Age Limit: 18-30 years old.
  • Payment Process:
    • 400 CNY (Application Fee) must be paid before applying.
    • 7,500 CNY (Tuition Fee) must be paid after receiving the admission letter.
    • 3,000 CNY (Accommodation) is paid upon arrival.
  • Academic Note: If providing a pre-graduation certificate, the graduation date must be before January 6, 2026.
  • Deadline: Application deadline is January 20, 2026.
  • Start Date: Classes will begin in March 2026.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

What is the English requirement for the foundation program?
You must provide proof of English proficiency, such as IELTS 5.5, TOEFL 70, or Duolingo 85. A 3-minute self-introduction video in English is also required.
What happens after I complete the foundation program?
After passing the final exams (Math, Physics, English, Chinese), you will receive a completion certificate and can be directly admitted to CAUC's English-taught bachelor programs (BBA or Communication Engineering) with a First or Second Class scholarship.
What is the difference between the First and Second Class scholarships?
Both cover full tuition and accommodation. The First Class Scholarship also includes a 1400 CNY monthly stipend.
What is the application timeline?
The application deadline is January 20, 2026. Classes are scheduled to begin in March 2026.
metaDescription: Apply for the March 2026 Foundation Program at the Civil Aviation University of China (CAUC). Leads to full scholarships for Bachelor's programs. Deadline: Jan 20, 2026.

title: Beijing Normal University (BNU) Zhuhai MBA/MPA Scholarships 2026
content:
Admissions Open — 2026 Intake Deadline: March 10, 2026 ⏳ calculating…

Beijing Normal University (BNU) Zhuhai MBA/MPA Scholarships 2026

Offered by the Belt and Road School (BRS) at the BNU Zhuhai Campus (Guangdong). These programs are designed for professionals seeking to capitalize on opportunities from the Belt and Road Initiative and the Greater Bay Area.

Master's Program Details (MBA & MPA) 🎓

MBA (Master of Business Administration)

Prepares students with the skills and knowledge for advancing careers in a fast-growing business environment.

  • Theoretical knowledge of business administration
  • Sound understanding of international business strategy
  • Social responsibility awareness
  • Knowledge of the Belt and Road Initiative & Greater Bay Area

MPA (Master of Public Administration)

Prepares students with cutting-edge philosophy and management skills to shape the future of public and management sectors.

  • Theory and skills of public administration & policy
  • Cross-cultural communication and management skills
  • Knowledge of the Belt and Road Initiative & Greater Bay Area

Full & Partial Scholarships Available 🏆

All applicants are automatically considered for scholarships based on academic merit.

Type A: CSC Scholarship

  • Tuition Fees Free
  • Hostel Fees Free
  • Insurance Fees Free
  • 3000 CNY/Month Living Allowance

Type B Scholarship

  • Tuition Fees Free
  • Hostel Fees Free
  • Insurance Fees Free
  • 2000 CNY/Month Living Allowance

Type C Scholarship

  • Tuition Fees Free
  • Insurance Fees Free
  • 900-1000 CNY/Month Living Allowance

Eligibility & Required Documents 📄

Eligibility Requirements

  • Non-Chinese citizen with a foreign passport.
  • Under 50 years old.
  • Bachelor’s degree or above.
  • Work experience (including internship) is required. One year or longer is preferred.
  • English Proficiency (for non-native speakers):
    • Medium of Instruction (MOI) Certificate
    • TOEFL 90 or IELTS 6.0

Required Documents

Application Form for International Students
Original notarized copy of highest degree certificate
Original notarized copy of transcripts
Two recommendation letters (Professor or Assoc. Professor)
English proficiency proof (MOI, IELTS, or TOEFL)
Study Plan and Research Proposal
Passport personal information page
Certificate of working experience (or internship)
Valid Certificate of Non-Criminal Record
Foreigner Medical Examination Form

Admission Process & Timeline 🗓️

Online Application: Apply between Nov 15, 2025 – Mar 10, 2026.
Pay Application Fee: Pay the 600 RMB application fee (non-refundable).
Mail Hard Copies: After passing preliminary review, mail hard copies of all documents to the Admission Office within 10 days.
Online Interview: Successful applicants will be invited for an online interview. Interviews are conducted in batches from Dec 2025 to Jun 2026.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is work experience mandatory?
Yes, work experience (including internships) is required. One year or more is preferred by the university.
What is the application fee?
The application fee is 600 RMB and is non-refundable.
Do I need to mail my documents?
Yes, after passing the initial online preliminary review, you must deliver the hard copies of all required documents to the Admission Office within 10 days.
metaDescription: Apply for the 2026 MBA or MPA program at Beijing Normal University (BNU) Zhuhai. Full CSC and university scholarships available. Deadline: March 10, 2026.

title: How to Apply for the CSC Type A Scholarship: The Complete Guide
content:
Scholarship Guide Program Type: Type A (Bilateral) Level: Bachelor, Master, PhD

How to Apply for the CSC Type A Scholarship: A Complete Guide

Applying for the CSC Type A Scholarship (also known as the Chinese Government Scholarship-Bilateral Program) is a systematic process. This is the most common and prestigious scholarship for international students pursuing degree programs in China.

Understanding CSC Type A

First, it's crucial to know what Type A is. This scholarship is managed by the China Scholarship Council (CSC) and requires a specific application channel.

  • Application Channel: You must apply through your home country's designated dispatching authorities, NOT directly to Chinese universities. This is the key difference from Type B.
  • Who it's for: International students nominated by their home countries for full-degree studies (Bachelor's, Master's, or PhD) in China.
  • Coverage: Typically includes a full tuition waiver, free accommodation, medical insurance, and a monthly living stipend.

Step-by-Step Application Guide 🗓️

The entire process, from start to finish, usually takes place between October and April for programs starting in September. Always check the official CSC website and your national authority for the exact annual deadlines.

Step 1: Preliminary Research & Preparation (October - December)

This is the most critical phase for a successful application.

  1. Identify Your Dispatching Authority: This is the most important first step. Find the organization in your country responsible for nominating candidates (e.g., Ministry of Education, national scholarship board, or your country's embassy).
  2. Choose Your Program and Universities: Use the CSC University and Program Search Tool. You can select up to 3 universities. Carefully review the program's language of instruction and requirements (HSK or IELTS/TOEFL).
  3. Contact Potential Supervisors (Highly Recommended): For Master's and PhD programs, finding a supervisor who provides an acceptance letter dramatically increases your chances.
Step 2: Prepare Your Application Documents (December - January)

Gather and prepare all required documents according to CSC specifications.

Core Required Documents:

  • CSC Online Application Form
  • Passport Copy (valid and clear)
  • Highest Degree Certificate (notarized copy) or Pre-graduation Certificate
  • Academic Transcripts (notarized copy)
  • Study or Research Plan (min. 500 words for Postgrad)
  • Two Letters of Recommendation (from professors)
  • Passport-sized Photo
  • Foreigner Physical Examination Form
  • Language Proficiency Certificates (HSK, IELTS, TOEFL, or MOI)

Additional Helpful Documents:

  • CV/Resume
  • Supervisor Acceptance Letter (if you have one)
  • Publications or Proof of Achievements
Step 3: Submit Applications (January - March)

This is a two-part process. You must complete both.

  1. Apply to Your National Dispatching Authority: Follow their specific application procedure (online portal, email, or post) and submit all documents to them before their deadline.
  2. Complete the CSC Online Application: Go to the "Study in China" website, create an account, and select Program Type: A. You will need the Agency Number provided by your national dispatching authority.
Step 4: The Nomination and Review Process (March - May)

Your national authority nominates top candidates to the CSC. The CSC then forwards applications to your chosen universities for academic review and final admission decisions.

Step 5: The Final Result (June - July)

The CSC conducts a final review and officially awards the scholarship. You can check your status on the CSC portal. Successful applicants will receive a package containing:

  • Admission Letter from the university.
  • Visa Application for Study in China (JW201 Form).
  • Scholarship Award Letter.
Step 6: Visa Application and Departure (July - August)

Take your Admission Letter, JW201 Form, and Passport to the Chinese Embassy or Consulate in your country to apply for a Student (X1) Visa. After getting your visa, book your flight and inform your university of your arrival date.

Crucial Tips for Success 🏆

  • Start Early! The entire process takes 6-9 months. Rushing leads to mistakes.
  • Read Instructions Carefully: Follow the guidelines from both the CSC and your national authority exactly. A small mistake can lead to disqualification.
  • Study Plan is Key: Invest time in writing a compelling, specific, and realistic study plan. It's your chance to showcase your passion and potential.
  • Choose Universities Wisely: Don't just apply to the top 3. Have a balanced list (one dream, one match, one safe).
  • Secure a Supervisor: For postgraduate studies, this is the single most effective way to stand out.
  • Check Your Email Regularly: All communication from the CSC and universities will be via email.

Good luck with your application!
metaDescription: A complete step-by-step guide on how to apply for the CSC Type A Scholarship (Bilateral Program). Learn about the process, required documents, and tips for success.
